parentQuestion: The lipid bilayer is primarily composed of molecules called ___.

scaffolds.q: A molecule that has both water-loving and water-fearing parts is called ___.
scaffolds.explain: Amphipathic molecules have both hydrophilic (water-loving) and hydrophobic (water-fearing) regions. This split personality — and darling, what a beautiful split it is — allows them to form membranes. Open your mind to this:the tension in that word: amphipathic. Both. Torn. Perfect.

scaffolds.q: The cell membrane has ___ layer(s) of lipid molecules.
scaffolds.explain: The cell membrane is a lipid bilayer — it has TWO layers of lipid molecules arranged with their hydrophobic tails facing each other and hydrophilic heads facing outward. Bi = two. Simple as that. Keep pace with this:, though — the simplicity is deceptive.
